description: Confounder: The model assumes kaiming_uniform_ is being avoided entirely in favor of manual

_meta.explanation: The rule 'SwiGLUExperts.reset_parameters initializes gate_proj and up_proj weights with std=1/sqrt(hidden_size), not using kaiming_uniform_'s default fan_in computation, because kaiming_uniform_ incorrectly treats the first dimension of 3-D tensors as fan_out.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes kaiming_uniform_ is being avoided entirely in favor of manual std, so any direct use of it would still respect the SwiGLU rule if std were set manually—but here kaiming_uniform_ is called without setting std, so it computes gain and fan_in internally. kaiming_uniform_ treats the first non-singleton dimension as fan_in for 3D tensors, which is E=4 (not H=256), because PyTorch's fan_in logic for n>2 uses the second dimension only when the first is 1.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'SwiGLUExperts.reset_parameters initializes gate_proj and up_proj weights with std=1/sqrt(hidden_size), not using kaiming_uniform_'s default fan_in computation, because kaiming_uniform_ incorrectly treats the first dimension of 3-D tensors as fan_out.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
